column4: Shows that a closed `demonstrated` result from deliberation meets the configured vote threshold.
Shows that a closed `no_majority` result occurs only after threshold failure plus one of the two executable closure conditions.
Shows that a closed `not_demonstrated` result from deliberation meets the configured vote threshold.
Shows that a live deliberation transition still leaves a next seated council voter.
Shows that deliberation can change votes, status, phase, and resolution without changing the case frame.
Successful initialization fixes the proposition, policy, and council member identities.
Shows that initialization starts with no admitted supplemental materials.
Shows that initialization starts in the intended opening-state structure.
Shows that deliberation starts from a clean council record: no votes, round one, and every member seated.
Successful initialization starts with an empty admitted-material record, so provenance holds immediately.
Shows that successful initialization cannot already produce a closed case.
Shows that initialization rejects duplicate council member identifiers.
Computes the explicit initial budget of a successfully initialized case.
Lifts the case-frame invariant from individual steps to whole runs that begin at successful initialization.
Append-only record growth composes across successful runs.
Turns the structural phase invariant into a simpler fairness summary.
Lifts demonstrated-outcome soundness to every reachable closed state.
Lifts no-majority soundness to every reachable closed state.
Lifts not-demonstrated outcome soundness to every reachable closed state.
Shows that every reachable state preserves unique council member identifiers.
Shows that every reachable state preserves the initialized council size.
Shows that every reachable state preserves distinct current-round votes from seated members and keeps vote rounds bounded.
Shows that flipping every current-round substantive vote flips the resolution the same way in every reachable state.
Shows that every reachable live deliberation state still has a next council voter.
Shows that cumulative exhibit and report caps hold in every reachable state.
Shows that the Lean engine never strands a reachable live case without a next opportunity.
Shows that successful initialization and later steps preserve a nonempty council roster.
Shows that every successful public execution preserves the merits sequence.
Shows that the phase and status views of closure stay aligned in every reachable state.
Derives side-to-side filing parity for every reachable state.
Every reachable state preserves the allowed phase-role origins of admitted materials.
Shows that seated council membership never exceeds the initialized council size.
Shows that deliberation rejects removal of a member who already voted in the current round.
Bounds the length of any successful public run from initialization by the initialized step budget.
Relates any successful public step path to the remaining-step budget of its start state.
Any successful public run changes admitted materials only by appending suffixes.
Once a member is non-seated, later successful public steps do not restore that member to seated.
Along any successful public run, the seated council roster can only shrink.
Reduces any closed demonstrated result at the public step boundary to the deliberation closure proof.
Reduces any closed no-majority result at the public step boundary to the deliberation closure proof.
Reduces any closed not-demonstrated result at the public step boundary to the deliberation closure proof.
Shows that every successful public step decreases the remaining-step budget.
Every successful public step leaves prior admitted materials intact and may append new ones.
Any newly introduced council vote comes from a member seated in the source state.
Every successful public step preserves the initialized case frame.
Every successful public step preserves the allowed origins of admitted materials.
Exposes the timed-out-member removal transition at the public step boundary.
Every successful public step leaves the seated roster unchanged or removes seated members.
Strengthens the budget theorem to a strict decrease at every successful public step.
Reduces the general new-vote theorem to the single council-vote action.
Exposes the council-vote transition at the public step boundary.
Shows that policy validation rejects thresholds that are not strict majorities.
